name: Painted Table Currant Scones
recipeIngredient: 2 Sticks (8 oz) Cold butter, cut into tablespoon-sized pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl combine the flour, sugar, baking powder, and salt.
2. Cut in butter with a pastry blender or with your fingers until the texture resembles coarse meal. Add currants and 2 cups of milk.
3. Lightly whisk eggs and cream together; add to dry ingredients. Combine with a large wooden spoon or mix by hand until dough barely holds together; do not overmix. (Dough should be slightly sticky, not dry. Add a little more cream if necessary.)
4. Grease a baking sheet or line with parchment paper.
5. Portion the dough using an ice cream scoop, or divide the dough in half and pat into 2 flattened disks. Cut each disk into 8 triangular shaped scones. Place on the prepared baking sheet and sprinkle the tops with sugar.
6. Bake in a preheated 375-degree oven about 20 minutes, until golden brown. Cool slightly before serving.
